{{Infobox group
| name = Enosh Travelers
| introduced = The Crimson Vault
}}

''See also: [[Enosh Travelers Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Enosh]] [[Travelers]] are individuals from the territory of [[Enosh]] who possess the ability to utilize [[Gates]] for travel. They are governed by [[Grandmasters]] and operate within a society that includes specialized waypoints, such as those found in [[Naraka]]. While they have historically contributed to the stability of their territories by managing roads, waystations, and wild elements, they have also been described as becoming increasingly aggressive. Some view them as a cult dedicated to the [[Incarnations]] with destructive intentions. In military contexts, they have been used to strike various locations by emerging from [[Gates]] to cause chaos before retreating. They are distinct from other groups, such as the Ragnarus or [[Damascan Travelers]], and [[Enosh]] does not possess [[Travelers]] from the Ragnarus [[Territory]].}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Driven by the goal to destroy the [[Tree]], the [[Enosh]] [[Travelers]] were responsible for the death of [[Simon]]'s father. This group utilized spinning, floating crystals that resembled wagon wheels to aid their movement. During [[Valin]]'s address to the people, both the travelers and the soldiers maintained a period of silence, though the group later showed respect toward [[Alin]]. As [[the Nye]] began attacking anyone in a [[Damascan]] uniform, they followed closely behind the [[Enosh]] [[Travelers]]. By the conclusion of the conflict, most of the travelers had either fled or perished, leaving those who remained to file through the Gate into the [[Crimson Vault]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Enosh Travelers]] aim to destroy the [[Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Enosh Travelers]] killed [[Simon]]'s father.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Enosh Travelers]] use floating crystals that spin like wagon wheels.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Enosh Travelers]] and soldiers remained silent while [[Valin]] addressed the people.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Enosh Travelers]] treated [[Alin]] with respect.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Nye]] were following the [[Enosh Travelers]] in attacking anything wearing a [[Damascan]] uniform.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Most of the [[Enosh Travelers]] were dead or had fled.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Enosh Travelers]] filed through the Gate to the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate groups]]
